methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Axiom Space is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company seeking valuation following $850M+ raised to date at a $2.5B post-money. The comp universe includes five public equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers, with four directly comparable Series C companies in the same sector: Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to the current $2.5B post-money, implying $4.2B. Peer-median anchoring across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because target revenue is not disclosed; while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 49.8x EV/Sales, private companies rarely disclose financials required for this method.

Triangulating the two applicable methods across low, median, and high scenarios produces an implied valuation range of $1.1B to $4.9B, with a median of $2.8B. The current $2.5B valuation sits near the lower end of this range. Key uncertainties include Axiom's revenue scale relative to peers—critical for validating the peer median—and the applicability of the 1.7x stage multiplier, which assumes typical venture progression rather than sector-specific growth dynamics.
